title: New Lower Bounds for Cap Sets

thm:1.2.summary: Cap-set lower bound improvement: there is an explicit cap set in $\mathbb{F}_3^{56232}$ of size $\binom{11}{7}^{141} \cdot 6^{572} \cdot 12^{572} \cdot 112^{8800} \cdot 37 \cdot 142$, hence for large $n$ a cap set $A \subseteq \mathbb{F}_3^n$ of size $(2.218021\ldots)^n$. Improves Edel's 2004 bound of $2.2^n$ — the first improvement in 18 years. The construction parameters $(11, 7)$, $(11, 6)$, $(10, 6)$ on admissible sets and the sizes $|A_0| = 12$, $|A_1| = |A_2| = 112$ in $\mathbb{F}_3^6$ are the levers FunSearch (Romera-Paredes et al., Nature 2024) later tunes.

def:1.1.summary: A cap set is $A \subseteq \mathbb{F}_3^n$ with no nontrivial solutions to $x + y + z = 0$ — equivalently no three distinct elements in arithmetic progression.

prop:2.1.summary: Product caps. If $A \subseteq \mathbb{F}_3^n$ and $B \subseteq \mathbb{F}_3^m$ are cap sets, then $A \times B \subseteq \mathbb{F}_3^{n+m}$ is a cap set of size $|A| |B|$. The trivial product construction underlying all cap-set lower bounds — exponentially additive in the exponent.

prop:2.2.summary: Asymptotic transfer. A cap set of size $c^n$ in some $\mathbb{F}_3^n$ implies that for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and large enough $m$ there is a cap set of size $> (c - \varepsilon)^m$ in $\mathbb{F}_3^m$. Lifts finite-dimensional constructions to asymptotic exponents via product:2.1.

def:2.4.summary: Extendable collection. A triple $(A_0, A_1, A_2)$ of cap sets in $\mathbb{F}_3^n$ is *extendable* if: (1) $x + y + z \ne 0$ whenever $x, y \in A_0$ and $z \in A_1 \cup A_2$, and (2) $x + y + z \ne 0$ whenever $x \in A_0$, $y \in A_1$, $z \in A_2$. The 'background' palette an admissible-set scheme will paint onto.

def:2.5.summary: Admissible set. $S \subseteq \{0, 1, 2\}^m$ is *admissible* if (1) for all distinct $s, s' \in S$ there are coordinates $i, j$ with $s_i = 0 \ne s_i'$ and $s_j \ne 0 = s_j'$, and (2) for all distinct $s, s', s'' \in S$ there is a coordinate $k$ with $\{s_k, s_k', s_k''\}$ equal to $\{0, 1, 2\}$, $\{0, 0, 1\}$, or $\{0, 0, 2\}$. This combinatorial object is the search target — finding larger admissible sets directly improves the cap-set lower bound, and is precisely what FunSearch's LLM-guided program search later optimizes over.

def:2.6.summary: Extended product construction. For $s \in \{0, 1, 2\}^m$, $s(A_0, A_1, A_2) := A_{s_1} \times \cdots \times A_{s_m} \subseteq \mathbb{F}_3^{nm}$; for $S \subseteq \{0,1,2\}^m$, $S(A_0, A_1, A_2) := \bigcup_{s \in S} s(A_0, A_1, A_2)$. The engine: glue together coordinate-wise products controlled by an admissible template $S$.

lem:2.7.summary: Extended construction yields cap set. If $(A_0, A_1, A_2)$ is extendable and $S$ is admissible, then $S(A_0, A_1, A_2)$ is a cap set in $\mathbb{F}_3^{nm}$. The cleanness of this lemma is what makes the construction modular: any admissible set times any extendable triple is a cap set, so improvement on either factor improves the bound.

def:2.8.summary: Recursively admissible. An admissible set with $|S| \ge 2$ satisfying the additional condition: for all distinct $s, s' \in S$, either (1) there are coordinates $i, j$ with $\{s_i, s_i'\} = \{0, 1\}$ and $\{s_j, s_j'\} = \{0, 2\}$, or (2) there is a coordinate $k$ with $s_k = s_k' = 0$. Refined structure that supports the recursive (meta-) construction in Section 3.

lem:2.11.summary: Counting formula. If $S = I(m, w) \subseteq \{0, 1, 2\}^m$ (the canonical admissible set with weight-$w$ pattern), and $|A_1| = |A_2|$, then $|S(A_0, A_1, A_2)| = \binom{m}{w} |A_0|^{m-w} |A_1|^{w}$. Exact size formula that turns the construction into an optimization problem over $(m, w, |A_0|, |A_1|)$.

lem:2.13.summary: Existence of specific admissible sets: $I(11, 7)$, $I(11, 6)$, and $I(10, 6)$ all exist as admissible subsets of $\{0,1,2\}^m$ at the appropriate dimensions. These specific witnesses are the combinatorial 'building blocks' that drive the $2.218^n$ bound. A SAT-solver search (CaDiCaL/Kissat) verifies the existence claims; FunSearch's improvement (later, Nature 2024) operates on the same search problem with LLM-guided program synthesis instead of pure SAT search.

lem:2.14.summary: Extendable triple in $\mathbb{F}_3^6$: there is an extendable collection $(A_0, A_1, A_2)$ with $|A_0| = 12$, $|A_1| = |A_2| = 112$. The 'palette' constants in the bound formula come from this lemma — the small-dimension search problem whose solution drives the asymptotic rate.

thm:2.15.summary: Intermediate cap-set construction. There exists a cap set $A \subseteq \mathbb{F}_3^{396}$ of size $\binom{11}{7} \cdot 6^4 \cdot 12^4 \cdot 112^{62}$. Combines lem:2.13 (admissible witness) with lem:2.14 (extendable palette) via the extended product construction. The stepping stone to thm:1.2 — applying meta-construction once more lifts dimensions to 56232.

prop:3.1.summary: Admissible sets are closed under direct product: if $S, T$ are admissible, so is $S \times T$. The natural multiplicative structure on the search space.

def:3.3.summary: Meta-admissible. A set $T \subseteq \{0, 1, 2\}^r$ is meta-admissible iff it is admissible in the sense of def:2.5 — same combinatorial conditions, applied one level up to operate on admissible-set palettes rather than on cap-set palettes.

def:3.4.summary: Meta-extendable. A collection $(S_0, S_1, S_2)$ of admissible sets is meta-extendable if (1) vectors in $S_0$ have strictly fewer nonzeros than vectors in $S_1 \cup S_2$, plus two coordinate-wise pattern conditions. The recursive analog of def:2.4 — palettes of palettes.

lem:3.6.summary: Meta-construction yields admissible. If $(S_0, S_1, S_2)$ is meta-extendable and $T$ is meta-admissible, then $T(S_0, S_1, S_2)$ is admissible. Lifts the extended product construction from cap-sets-by-admissible to admissibles-by-meta-admissible — the recursion that lets the construction be iterated.

prop:4.1.summary: Optimal admissible-set choice. For an extendable triple $(A_0, A_1, A_2)$ with $|A_1| = |A_2|$, the best admissible templates are $I(m, m\alpha)$ with $\alpha = |A_1| / (|A_0| + |A_1|)$ for large $m$. The asymptotic constant achievable via the extended product construction is $c = (|A_0| + |A_1|)^{1/n}$. Reveals the exact optimization landscape FunSearch operates within.

conjecture:4.3.summary: If $I(m, w)$ admissible sets exist for all $m > w > 0$, then cap sets in $\mathbb{F}_3^n$ have size at least $124^{n/6} \approx 2.233^n$. Identifies the next milestone above $2.218^n$ — a specific combinatorial conjecture FunSearch later attempts.

def:5.1.summary: Boolean satisfiability problem (SAT). The decision problem of whether a propositional formula admits a true-making assignment. Tyrrell's existence proofs for admissible sets (lem:2.13) and extendable triples (lem:2.14) are reduced to SAT instances and solved via CaDiCaL/Kissat solvers — a key methodological ingredient that FunSearch generalizes via LLM-guided program search.

edges.note: thm:1.2 lifts thm:2.15's $\mathbb{F}_3^{396}$ construction to $\mathbb{F}_3^{56232}$ via meta-construction in Section 3.
Meta-construction (lem:3.6) is what lifts the inner cap-set construction by another factor.

cap_set_construction_framework.description: The extended product construction: a cap set is built by gluing copies of an extendable triple $(A_0, A_1, A_2)$ according to an admissible template $S$.

computer_search_witnesses.description: Specific admissible sets $I(11,7)$, $I(11,6)$, $I(10,6)$ and an extendable triple in $\mathbb{F}_3^6$ with sizes $(12, 112, 112)$, found via SAT-solver search. These are the search targets that FunSearch later improves via LLM-guided program synthesis.

intermediate_construction.description: Combine the SAT witnesses via the extended product construction to get a cap set in $\mathbb{F}_3^{396}$.

meta_recursion.description: Meta-admissible and meta-extendable structures lift the construction one level up — admissible sets are built from meta-admissible templates over meta-extendable palettes.

final_bound_and_optimization.description: Iterate the meta-construction to reach $\mathbb{F}_3^{56232}$ and extract the asymptotic rate $2.218021^n$. Identifies the optimization landscape FunSearch operates on.
